findings.message: New mutating endpoint archive_item is added with no business-event observability: no structured log on archive, no metric or span attribute identifying the archive operation, and no trace context propagation beyond what the global middleware happens to provide. A state-changing route should emit an info-level structured log (with correlation id and item_id) and/or a dedicated counter so the archive action is observable independently of generic request metrics.
findings.suggestion: Inject the project logger (e.g. demo.logging_config.get_logger) and emit log.info("item_archived", item_id=item_id, correlation_id=<from request>) after session.commit(); optionally bump a dedicated counter (e.g. app_items_archived_total) on the metrics registry.

findings.message: The 404 error path (item not found) raises HTTPException without logging it with the request's correlation id. Error paths must be logged with the correlation id so the not-found rate on this endpoint is observable and traceable.
findings.suggestion: Before raising HTTPException, log a warn-level structured event, e.g. log.warning("archive_item_not_found", item_id=item_id, correlation_id=<from request>).

findings.message: No SLO threshold is defined or referenced for this new write endpoint. The existing SLOs (request_latency_p99_ms, error_rate_pct in observability/slo.py) are global; a state-mutating archive endpoint should have an explicit per-endpoint SLO (latency and/or success-rate) and a corresponding alert/dashboard panel, otherwise regressions on /items/{item_id}/archive go unnoticed.
findings.suggestion: Add a per-route SLO (e.g. archive_item_latency_p99_ms / archive_item_error_rate_pct) to default_slos and map its PromQL in observability/provisioning.py so the generated Prometheus alert rules and Grafana dashboard cover it.

findings.message: The mutation (item.name update + session.commit) is performed without a dedicated span or span attributes (e.g. setting item_id on the current span). With only the FastAPI auto-instrumentation, the trace shows a generic POST span but not the archived item id, hurting traceability when investigating user reports.
findings.suggestion: Acquire the current span (opentelemetry.trace.get_current_span()) and call set_attribute("item.id", item_id) / set_attribute("app.operation", "archive_item"), or create an explicit child span around the commit.

findings.message: Importing HTTPException lazily inside the function body (from fastapi import HTTPException) is unusual and obscures the error path from static analysis and from any import-time observability wiring; prefer a module-level import alongside APIRouter/Depends.
findings.suggestion: Move `from fastapi import HTTPException` to the top-level imports.
